result.overall_plan: The circuit creates a 90-degree signal path from a lever to a redstone lamp. The signal flows from the lever, through a straight line of wire, turns at the corner, and terminates at the lamp, which will toggle based on the lever state.

construction_steps.reasoning: Placing the lever at the origin acts as the primary input for the circuit. It is the dependency for all downstream components as it initiates the redstone signal.
Placing the wire at (1,0,0) connects directly to the lever at (0,0,0) to propagate the signal forward along the X-axis.
This wire continues the signal path from (1,0,0) towards the planned corner at (3,0,0).
This wire acts as the junction point. It receives power from (2,0,0) and redirects the signal flow along the Z-axis toward the lamp.
This wire extends the signal from the junction at (3,0,0) to continue the path, ensuring the signal reaches the lamp at the end of the line.
This final segment of wire is placed to bridge the gap between the wire at (3,0,1) and the lamp at (3,0,3), completing the circuit connection.
The lamp is placed at the end of the wire line to serve as the output device. It depends on the signal transmitted through the wires at (3,0,2) to function.
